rationale: The citing paper explicitly positions itself as extending prior default-effects research from one-shot choices to sequential dynamics, and the cited paper is a defaults-focused JMR study on mechanisms/interventions (disclosure and preference articulation) in one-shot settings. Given this strong topical and conceptual overlap (defaults, process underlying default influence), the citation is plausibly foundational/comparative rather than an obvious incidental mention. Although the raw match provides only DOI-level evidence, there is not enough basis to safely drop.
